surface.name: Qdrant Cloud free cluster through Python semantic search with Cloud Inference
surface.selection_basis: The official Cloud Quickstart explicitly promises the first semantic search in a few minutes, uses a free cluster and a free Cloud Inference model, and ends with a visible list of semantically matched menu items. The email route requires only an email address and a one-time authorization code. However, the general Managed Cloud guide says payment details are required before creating a cluster, while the free Quickstart and pricing page do not include a payment step or resolve whether that prerequisite applies to Free Tier.

alternatives_considered.name: Local Docker quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is self-hosted and requires Docker, while the roster platform is the managed Qdrant Cloud service.

alternatives_considered.name: Google or GitHub signup
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These routes add third-party authentication and consent, while the official privacy policy says email registration requires only email and an authorization code.

alternatives_considered.name: Integrated Cluster UI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The selected Cloud Quickstart uses an official SDK and explicitly culminates in semantic search; the UI is a peer access surface.

alternatives_considered.name: REST or gRPC without an SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Quickstart calls official SDKs the fastest way to begin and provides a complete Python route.

alternatives_considered.name: Standard paid cluster
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The free tier is labeled free forever and already includes the selected free inference model; Standard adds usage charges and sizing.

alternatives_considered.name: External embedding provider
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This adds a separate provider API key and billing, while selected Cloud Inference models are free on the free cluster.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Qdrant Cloud account and free cluster, then use the official Python Cloud Quickstart to load the published menu dataset and perform the first semantic search.
assumed_prior_state: No Qdrant Cloud user, account, cluster, database API key, endpoint, collection, billing instrument, local client, embedding provider, or data is assumed.
The developer has an email address and access to its inbox for passwordless authorization.
The developer can choose a preferred supported cloud provider and region for the free cluster.
Python and pip are available locally; installation was not executed during research.
No account, authorization code, cluster, key, package installation, API request, collection, point, or query was created during research.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer loads the official menu-item dataset, embeds it with a free Qdrant Cloud Inference model, and performs the published semantic search for vegetarian dishes.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Python program prints up to five menu items with item name, similarity score, description, and price; the guide then confirms that semantic search on real menu-item data was performed.
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Cloud Quickstart is explicitly framed around the first semantic search and ends this sequence with printed ranked results plus the guide's `That’s Vector Search!` confirmation. Filtering, advanced collections, other tutorials, and production preparation are listed afterward.

prerequisites.requirement: An email address and access to its inbox; official policy says email registration requires only the address and sends an authorization code.
Choose a cluster name, supported cloud provider, and preferred region for the free cluster.
Copy the generated database API key at its one-time display and use the cluster URL from the console.
Have Python and pip to install and run the official qdrant-client package.
Use the free `sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` model exposed through Qdrant Cloud Inference.
Use of the selected free inference model places inference in the US because Qdrant states that free models are hosted in the US region only.

primary_path.action: Open Quick Start with Qdrant Cloud and follow its Cloud account link.
primary_path.output: The Qdrant Cloud registration interface opens.
primary_path.success_signal: Email, Google, and GitHub registration routes are available as documented.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No navigation failure or recovery behavior is documented.

primary_path.action: Choose the email route and enter the email address.
primary_path.output: Qdrant sends an authorization code to the address.
primary_path.success_signal: The authorization email is the documented next state; no password or other registration input is required.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No delivery time, resend procedure, code lifetime, or invalid-code behavior is documented.

primary_path.action: Open the Qdrant email and use its authorization code to complete passwordless registration and login.
primary_path.success_signal: The Create a Free Cluster area is available as the next Quickstart state.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The official policy establishes the emailed authorization code but does not publish code-error recovery.

primary_path.action: Under Create a Free Cluster, enter a cluster name and select the preferred cloud provider and region.
primary_path.input: Cluster name, cloud provider, and region
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide does not publish unavailable-name, provider, or region recovery. A separate current Managed Cloud guide says payment details are required before cluster creation, but the Free Tier pages do not resolve whether that cost gate applies here.

primary_path.output: Qdrant deploys the cluster and prompts with a database API key.
primary_path.success_signal: The API key is displayed and the cluster detail page provides its endpoint.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The product page says console deployment takes only a few seconds but publishes no timeout or retry sequence.

primary_path.action: Copy the API key immediately and store it securely because it will not be displayed again; copy the cluster URL from Cluster Details.
primary_path.input: Generated API key and cluster endpoint
primary_path.output: The developer has the two connection values required by the client.
primary_path.success_signal: The key and cluster URL are available for the Python connection constructor.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The Quickstart documents one-time display but does not publish recovery from a lost first key on this page.

primary_path.action: Install the official Python SDK with `pip install qdrant-client`.
primary_path.success_signal: The QdrantClient and model classes can be imported in the next code block.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No install failure or package-version recovery is documented in the Quickstart.

primary_path.action: Create a Python file, import QdrantClient plus Distance, VectorParams, PointStruct, and Document, then instantiate the client with the cluster URL, API key, and `cloud_inference=True`.
primary_path.output: The client is configured to connect to the cluster and use Qdrant Cloud Inference.
primary_path.success_signal: The same client can issue the collection, upsert, and query calls that follow.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide gives no Python connection-error recovery; a curl collection-list request is provided as a peer connection test.

primary_path.action: Call `create_collection` for `items` with vector size 384 and cosine distance.
primary_path.success_signal: The collection is ready for the published points.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No collection-creation failure or duplicate-name recovery is documented in the Quickstart.

primary_path.action: Copy the published 30-menu-item dataset, build a PointStruct for each row, and pass the item name plus description to a Document using `sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` while retaining name, description, price, and category as payload.
primary_path.input: Thirty published menu records and the free embedding model
primary_path.output: Thirty points are prepared for server-side embedding and insertion.
primary_path.success_signal: The points list contains the official menu data and Document vector inputs.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide does not publish an embedding-construction failure or recovery sequence.

primary_path.action: Call `client.upsert` with collection `items` and the prepared points.
primary_path.output: Qdrant Cloud Inference embeds the text and Qdrant stores the points in `items`.
primary_path.success_signal: The published search code can query the populated collection.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No upsert failure or retry behavior is documented in the Quickstart.

primary_path.action: Set `query_text` to `vegetarian dishes` and call `query_points` on `items` with a Document using the same model, payload enabled, and limit 5.
primary_path.input: Published natural-language query, model, collection, and limit
primary_path.output: Qdrant embeds the query and returns the nearest menu items.
primary_path.success_signal: The result object contains scored points with payload.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No query failure or retry behavior is documented in the Quickstart.

primary_path.action: Run the Python file and print each result's item name, score, description, and price.
primary_path.output: Up to five semantically similar menu items are printed.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide confirms: the developer has performed semantic search on real menu-item data, and the query returned similar items based on meaning rather than keywords.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide publishes the positive completion statement but no terminal-specific recovery behavior.

candidate_paths.name: Create a free Qdrant Cloud cluster and run the Python menu semantic search
candidate_paths.selection_basis: Official registration policy resolves the email-only passwordless account route, and the Cloud Quickstart specifies cluster inputs, one-time key, SDK, collection, data, query, and observable result. The candidate remains conditional because separate current official guidance says payment details are required before cluster creation without stating whether Free Tier is exempt.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: This is the official managed Cloud Quickstart, uses the free tier and free Cloud Inference, and stops at its first stated semantic-search milestone.

branches.path: Authenticate with an active Google account and consent to release the provider information needed for registration.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the email authorization code with Google SSO, then converges on free-cluster creation.

branches.path: Authenticate with an active GitHub account and consent to release the provider information needed for registration.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the email authorization code with GitHub SSO, then converges on free-cluster creation.

branches.path: Select the preferred supported combination; Qdrant Cloud is available on AWS, Azure, and GCP.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes cluster location and endpoint but not the Quickstart code structure.

branches.condition: The general Managed Cloud payment prerequisite applies to Free Tier
branches.path: Provide payment details through a credit card or an AWS, GCP, or Azure Marketplace subscription before cluster creation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a billing instrument or marketplace procurement step that the Free Tier Quickstart and pricing page omit; current official pages do not resolve applicability.

branches.condition: Rust, JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, C#, Go, or bash
branches.path: Install the corresponding official client or use curl and follow that language tab's collection, population, and query code.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes local setup and printed-output code while preserving the same cluster, data, model, and semantic-search terminal.

branches.path: Open the Cluster UI from Cluster Details; authentication is automatic when the cloud user has read or write cluster-data permission, otherwise enter an API key.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses the web interface instead of the selected SDK route and does not follow the published menu program literally.

branches.path: Select a paid model from the Inference tab and incur token-based charges.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a model-cost choice that the selected free model avoids.

branches.path: Supply an OpenAI, Cohere, Jina AI, or OpenRouter API key through Qdrant Cloud Inference.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds third-party credentials and provider billing before the same data and query sequence.

branches.path: Configure a payment method or commercial agreement and create paid resources instead of Create Free Cluster.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds billing, sizing, and possibly support or SSO choices that are not needed for the free Quickstart.

friction_gates.description: The email route depends on receiving and using a passwordless authorization code; no delivery time, expiry, resend, or invalid-code recovery is documented.
Free-cluster creation requires a preferred cloud provider and region; the Quickstart does not prescribe a universal best location.

Official guidance conflicts on whether payment details are required before the free cluster: the general guide requires them before cluster creation, while the Free Tier route omits them and is labeled free forever.
Qdrant's product page says console deployment takes only a few seconds but publishes no timeout or recovery sequence.
The initial database API key will not be shown again, so the documented prevention is to copy it and store it safely when prompted.

The current free and paid model list is exposed in the cluster's Inference tab; the selected model is explicitly identified as free in the Quickstart, and Qdrant states that free models are hosted in the US region only.
If data growth exceeds 1 GB RAM or 4 GB disk, the documented recovery is to upgrade to Standard and scale the cluster.

time_to_first_success.value: The Cloud Quickstart says the developer can set up Qdrant Cloud and perform the first semantic search in just a few minutes, while the product page says console cluster deployment takes only a few seconds. Neither is a measured end-to-end duration.

sources.title: Setting up a Qdrant Cloud Account
Privacy Policy
sections_used: Registration for and use of the Qdrant Cloud Service
evidence_supported: Google and GitHub consent and data transfer

sources.title: Inference in Qdrant Managed Cloud
evidence_supported: Selected free models on free clusters
Free models hosted in the US region only

sources.title: Getting Started with Qdrant Managed Cloud

uncertainties.question: Must a developer provide payment details before creating the Free Tier cluster?
uncertainties.impact: A credit card or marketplace subscription would add a material cost and procurement gate before the selected first-mile route.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current Cloud Quickstart omits payment and pricing labels Free Tier free forever, while the general Managed Cloud guide says payment details are required before creating a cluster; the billing guide does not state a Free Tier exemption.

uncertainties.question: How long is the email authorization code valid and how is a missing or invalid code recovered?
uncertainties.impact: Email delivery or code failure can add an unknown wait before free-cluster creation.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Official policy states that the code is emailed but publishes no timing or recovery details.

uncertainties.question: Which supported provider and region should a developer choose?
uncertainties.impact: The endpoint location and data residency vary, but all documented technical steps remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Quickstart intentionally asks for the developer's preferred provider and region rather than prescribing one.

uncertainties.question: What exact ranked item names and scores will the free inference model return at execution time?
uncertainties.impact: The observable rows may vary with model or service updates, but the success criterion remains a nonempty printed set of scored menu items.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Quickstart publishes the input data and print fields but no immutable sample output values.

excluded_after_success: Filtering, advanced collection configuration, other examples and tutorials, and the Production Checklist are listed after the first semantic search.

Paid cluster usage, additional accounts or users, enterprise SSO, external models, backups, scaling, monitoring, and production hardening are outside the selected free first-mile path; whether payment details are nevertheless required before Free Tier creation remains unresolved above.
